conservationOrWildlifeManagement.comments: The organism was released with the intention of improving the conservation status of the species or the conservation status other species in the habitat. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

releasedForUse.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p007
releasedForUse.dc:description: Release in nature for use (other than above, e.g., fur, transport, medical use).
releasedForUse.comments: This term refers to organisms intentionally and directly released into the wild to serve a specific purpose. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

otherIntentionalRelease.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p008
otherIntentionalRelease.dc:description: A catch-all term for intentional releases not for human use that are not covered by other more specific terms.
otherIntentionalRelease.comments: Compare with "other escape from confinement". See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

agriculture.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p009
agriculture.dc:description: Plants grown with the intention of harvesting.
agriculture.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

aquacultureMariculture.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p010
aquacultureMariculture.dc:description: The analog of agriculture and farmed animals, specifically related to aquatic organisms.
aquacultureMariculture.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

publicGardenZooAquaria.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p011
publicGardenZooAquaria.dc:description: Organisms in public collections of plants and/or animals.
publicGardenZooAquaria.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

pet.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p012
pet.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129
names: pet/aquarium/terrarium species (including live food for such species)

farmedAnimals.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p013
farmedAnimals.dc:description: Animals cared for and bred with the specific intention of using their products, such as meat and milk.
farmedAnimals.comments: Farmed animals are generally kept in a defined area, such as a fields. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129
names: farmed animals (including animals left under limited control)

forestry.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p014
forestry.dc:description: Trees specifically introduced to provide timber and other forestry products.
forestry.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

fur.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p015
fur.dc:description: Organisms escaped from a fur farm, including unauthorised releases.
fur.comments: Probably only applicable to vertebrates raised for their pelts and skins. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

horticulture.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p016
horticulture.dc:description: Plants distributed by the ornamental and decorative plants industry.
horticulture.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

ornamentalNonHorticulture.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p017
ornamentalNonHorticulture.dc:description: Ornamental plants introduced through pathways other than the horticultural industry.
ornamentalNonHorticulture.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

research.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p018
research.dc:description: Plants and animals introduced for the purpose of breeding or scientific and medical research, including science education.
research.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

liveFoodLiveBait.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p019
liveFoodLiveBait.dc:description: Live food imported for human consumption or live bait, such as shellfish and snails.
liveFoodLiveBait.comments: Live food, such as mealworms, for the organisms kept as pets should be classified under the pet term. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

otherEscape.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p020
otherEscape.dc:description: Organisms brought into an area with the intention of keeping them in captivity permanently, but that have subsequently escaped.
otherEscape.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

contaminantNursery.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p021
contaminantNursery.dc:description: Organisms transported into an area together with plant material.
contaminantNursery.comments: These may be other plants, diseases, fungi and animals. They may be attached to the plant or within the soil. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

contaminateBait.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p022
contaminateBait.dc:description: Contaminants, pathogens and parasites transported with live, frozen or preserved bait used to catch fish or other organisms.
contaminateBait.comments: Typical examples include crustaceans, cephalopods and molluscs. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

foodContaminant.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p023
foodContaminant.dc:description: Foods for human consumption, whether they are transported live or dead.
foodContaminant.comments: This term includes unintentional introduction of contaminants such as diseases on those foods and in the case of plants, should include seeds. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

contaminantOnAnimals.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p024
contaminantOnAnimals.dc:description: Contaminants carried either on or in the body of transported animals.
contaminantOnAnimals.comments: Transported animals carry other organisms in their coats, in their guts and in soil on their hooves and feet. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129
names: contaminant on animals (except parasites, organisms transported by host/vector)

parasitesOnAnimals.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p025
parasitesOnAnimals.dc:description: Parasitic and pathogenic organisms transported with their host or vector animal.
parasitesOnAnimals.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129
names: parasites on animals (including organisms transported by host and vector)

contaminantOnPlants.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p026
contaminantOnPlants.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129
names: contaminant on plants (except parasites, species transported by host/vector)

parasitesOnPlants.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p027
parasitesOnPlants.dc:description: Parasitic and pathogenic organisms transported with their host or vector plant.
parasitesOnPlants.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129
names: parasites on plants (including species transported by host and vector)

seedContaminant.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p028
seedContaminant.comments: These may be parasites or pathogens of seeds or species that eat seeds, whether intended to be transported or not. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

timberTrade.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p029
timberTrade.dc:description: Contaminants on unprocessed timber, processed wood and wood-derived products.
timberTrade.comments: Examples include wooden furniture, saw dust and fire wood. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

transportationHabitatMaterial.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p030
transportationHabitatMaterial.dc:description: Organisms transported with their habitat material to a new location.
transportationHabitatMaterial.comments: Examples include materials such as soil, vegetation, straw and wood chips. Unless these materials are sterilised the organisms can be transported with their habitat to a new location. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129
names: transportation of habitat material (soil, vegetation, wood etc)

fishingEquipment.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p031
fishingEquipment.dc:description: Aquatic organisms moved between sites on equipment of recreational anglers and professional fishermen.
fishingEquipment.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

containerBulk.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p032
containerBulk.dc:description: Stowaways transported in or on cargo containers or bulk cargo units.
containerBulk.comments: The difference between this category and others, such as "hitchhikers on ship/boat", is that the organism embarked and disembarked from the container rather than the ship. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

hitchhikersAirplane.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p033
hitchhikersAirplane.dc:description: Organisms that enter airplanes or other aircraft, such as helicopters, and are transported by them to another location.
hitchhikersAirplane.comments: This term does not apply to organisms that embarked in containers that were subsequently loaded onto an aircraft. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

hitchhikersShip.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p034
hitchhikersShip.dc:description: Organisms that enter directly onto boats or ships and are transported by them to another location.
hitchhikersShip.comments: This term does not apply to organisms that embarked in containers that are subsequently loaded onto the ship, nor to contaminants of products loaded onto the ship. The term is intended for organisms that directly interact with the boat or ship. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129
names: hitchhikers on ship/boat (excluding ballast water and hull fouling)

machineryEquipment.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p035
machineryEquipment.dc:description: Organisms carried on the surfaces of or within heavy machinery and equipment.
machineryEquipment.comments: This includes military equipment, farm machinery and manufacturing equipment. This term does not include products carried by vehicles. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

people.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p036
people.dc:description: Organisms transported on people and/or their personal luggage.
people.comments: Examples include organisms transported by tourists. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129
names: people and their luggage/equipment (in particular tourism)

packingMaterial.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p037
packingMaterial.dc:description: Organic material, particularly unprocessed plant material that is used to pack transported goods.
packingMaterial.comments: Examples include woodern pallets, boxes, bags and baskets. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129
names: organic packing material, in particular wood packaging

ballastWater.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p038
ballastWater.dc:description: Organisms transported within the water pumped into boats and ships to provide ballast.
ballastWater.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

hullFouling.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p039
hullFouling.dc:description: Organisms that attach themselves to the subsurface hull of boats and ships.
hullFouling.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

vehicles.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p040
vehicles.dc:description: Other vehicle hitchhikers that have been unintentionally dispersed, but are not covered by other terms.
vehicles.comments: These organisms may be carried on or within the vehicle. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

otherTransport.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p041
otherTransport.dc:description: A catch-all term for any transport related dispersal that is not covered in other terms.
otherTransport.comments: Examples include fouling from offshore oil and gas platforms, offshore renewable energy sites (such as wind farms, pipelines, cable transport, etc.) See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

waterwaysBasinsSeas.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p042
waterwaysBasinsSeas.dc:description: Organisms that dispersed through artificial waterways created to connect previosuly unconnected water bodies.
waterwaysBasinsSeas.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

tunnelsBridges.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p043
tunnelsBridges.dc:description: Unintentional dispersal by organisms using artificial tunnels, bridges, roads and railways.
tunnelsBridges.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

naturalDispersal.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p044
naturalDispersal.dc:description: Dispersal of organisms to new regions by natural dispersal from regions in which they are alien.
naturalDispersal.comments: These are alien species that have previously been introduced through one of these pathways: release in nature, escape from confinement, transport-contaminant, transport-stowaway, or corridor. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129
names: natural dispersal across borders of invasive alien organisms

releaseInNature.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p045
releaseInNature.dc:description: Organisms transported and released by humans in a (semi)natural environment with the intention that they should live there without further human aid.
releaseInNature.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

escapeFromConfinement.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p046
escapeFromConfinement.dc:description: Organisms intentionally transported by humans and intended to be kept in captivity or cultivation, but having inadvertently escaped from human control.
escapeFromConfinement.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

transportContaminant.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p047
transportContaminant.dc:description: An umbrella term for all species transported as contaminants in other products.
transportContaminant.comments: An alien species is a contaminant if it had a trophic or biotic relationship to organisms or items being transported and was to some extent dependent on them for survival. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

transportStowaway.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p048
transportStowaway.dc:description: An umbrella term for all species transported by riding on forms of transport where the organism has a direct interaction with the transport and is not merely carried as part of, or a contaminant of cargo.
transportStowaway.comments: A stowaway has no trophic or biotic relationship to the organisms or items being transported. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

corridor.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p049
corridor.dc:description: Infrastructure, such as bridges, tunnels and canals have removed natural barriers to dispersal and allowed a species to move into a novel location.
corridor.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

unaided.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p050
unaided.dc:description: Organisms that spread by natural dispersal, without action or assistance by humans, from regions in which they are also alien.
unaided.comments: The term refers to secondary dispersal from an area where the taxon is also alien. See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

intentional.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p051
intentional.dc:description: Organisms were brought to new area with the specific intention of keeping them alive in the new region, regardless of whether they were intended to be cultivated or released into the wild.
intentional.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

unintentional.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p052
unintentional.dc:description: The organism was unintentionally brought to a new region.
unintentional.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

corridorAndDispersal.dc:subject: http://rs.tdwg.org/dwcpw/values/p053
corridorAndDispersal.dc:description: Organisms dispersed naturally, even if that dispersal was aided by changes in the landscape created by humans.
corridorAndDispersal.comments: See also Harrower et al. 2017. http://nora.nerc.ac.uk/id/eprint/519129

georeferenceVerificationStatus.name: georeferenceVerificationStatus
georeferenceVerificationStatus.label: Georeference Verification Status
georeferenceVerificationStatus.dc:description: A categorical description of the extent to which the georeference has been verified to represent the best possible spatial description for the dcterms:Location of the dwc:Occurrence.
georeferenceVerificationStatus.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary. This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.
georeferenceVerificationStatus.examples: `unable to georeference`; `requires georeference`; `requires verification`; `verified by data custodian`; `verified by contributor`

occurrenceStatus.name: occurrenceStatus
occurrenceStatus.label: Occurrence Status
occurrenceStatus.dc:description: A statement about the presence or absence of a dwc:Taxon at a dcterms:Location.
occurrenceStatus.comments: For dwc:Occurrences, the default vocabulary is recommended to consist of `present` and `absent`, but can be extended by implementers with good justification. This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

present.dc:description: The occurrence was present at the location and time of the observation.

absent.dc:description: The occurrence was not present at the location and time of the observation.

associatedMedia.name: associatedMedia
associatedMedia.label: Associated Media
associatedMedia.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of identifiers (publication, global unique identifier, URI) of media associated with the dwc:Occurrence.

associatedOccurrences.name: associatedOccurrences
associatedOccurrences.label: Associated Occurrences
associatedOccurrences.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of identifiers of other dwc:Occurrence records and their associations to this dwc:Occurrence.
associatedOccurrences.comments: This term can be used to provide a list of associations to other dwc:Occurrences. Note that the dwc:ResourceRelationship class is an alternative means of representing associations, and with more detail. Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space (` | `).
associatedOccurrences.examples: `"parasite collected from":"https://arctos.database.museum/guid/MSB:Mamm:215895?seid=950760"`; `"encounter previous to":"http://arctos.database.museum/guid/MSB:Mamm:292063?seid=3175067" | "encounter previous to":"http://arctos.database.museum/guid/MSB:Mamm:292063?seid=3177393" | "encounter previous to":"http://arctos.database.museum/guid/MSB:Mamm:292063?seid=3177394" | "encounter previous to":"http://arctos.database.museum/guid/MSB:Mamm:292063?seid=3177392" | "encounter previous to":"http://arctos.database.museum/guid/MSB:Mamm:292063?seid=3609139"`

associatedReferences.name: associatedReferences
associatedReferences.label: Associated References
associatedReferences.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of identifiers (publication, bibliographic reference, global unique identifier, URI) of literature associated with the dwc:Occurrence.
associatedReferences.comments: Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space (` | `). Note that the dwc:ResourceRelationship class is an alternative means of representing associations, and with more detail. Note also that the intended usage of the term dcterms:references in Darwin Core when applied to a dwc:Occurrence is to point to the definitive source representation of that dwc:Occurrence if one is available. Note also that the intended usage of dcterms:bibliographicCitation in Darwin Core when applied to a dwc:Occurrence is to provide the preferred way to cite the dwc:Occurrence itself.
associatedReferences.examples: `http://www.sciencemag.org/cgi/content/abstract/322/5899/261`; `Christopher J. Conroy, Jennifer L. Neuwald. 2008. Phylogeographic study of the California vole, Microtus californicus Journal of Mammalogy, 89(3):755-767.`; `Steven R. Hoofer and Ronald A. Van Den Bussche. 2001. Phylogenetic Relationships of Plecotine Bats and Allies Based on Mitochondrial Ribosomal Sequences. Journal of Mammalogy 82(1):131-137. | Walker, Faith M., Jeffrey T. Foster, Kevin P. Drees, Carol L. Chambers. 2014. Spotted bat (Euderma maculatum) microsatellite discovery using illumina sequencing. Conservation Genetics Resources.`

associatedTaxa.name: associatedTaxa
associatedTaxa.label: Associated Taxa
associatedTaxa.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of identifiers or names of dwc:Taxon records and the associations of this dwc:Occurrence to each of them.
associatedTaxa.comments: This term can be used to provide a list of associations to dwc:Taxon records other than the one defined in the dwc:Occurrence. Note that the dwc:ResourceRelationship class is an alternative means of representing associations, and with more detail. This term is not apt for establishing relationships between dwc:Taxon records, only between specific dwc:Occurrences of a dwc:Organism with other dwc:Taxon records. Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space (` | `).

otherCatalogNumbers.name: otherCatalogNumbers
otherCatalogNumbers.label: Other Catalog Numbers
otherCatalogNumbers.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of previous or alternate fully qualified catalog numbers or other human-used identifiers for the same dwc:Occurrence, whether in the current or any other data set or collection.
otherCatalogNumbers.comments: Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space (` | `).

occurrenceRemarks.name: occurrenceRemarks
occurrenceRemarks.label: Occurrence Remarks
occurrenceRemarks.dc:description: Comments or notes about the dwc:Occurrence.

organismID.name: organismID
organismID.label: Organism ID
organismID.dc:description: An identifier for the dwc:Organism instance (as opposed to a particular digital record of the dwc:Organism). May be a globally unique identifier or an identifier specific to the data set.

organismName.name: organismName
organismName.label: Organism Name
organismName.dc:description: A textual name or label assigned to a dwc:Organism instance.

organismScope.name: organismScope
organismScope.label: Organism Scope
organismScope.dc:description: A description of the kind of dwc:Organism instance. Can be used to indicate whether the dwc:Organism instance represents a discrete organism or if it represents a particular type of aggregation.
organismScope.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary. This term is not intended to be used to specify a type of dwc:Taxon. To describe the kind of dwc:Organism using a URI object in RDF, use rdf:type (http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type) instead.

associatedOrganisms.name: associatedOrganisms
associatedOrganisms.label: Associated Organisms
associatedOrganisms.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of identifiers of other dwc:Organisms and the associations of this dwc:Organism to each of them.
associatedOrganisms.comments: This term can be used to provide a list of associations to other dwc:Organisms. Note that the dwc:ResourceRelationship class is an alternative means of representing associations, and with more detail. Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space (` | `).

previousIdentifications.name: previousIdentifications
previousIdentifications.label: Previous Identifications
previousIdentifications.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of previous assignments of names to the dwc:Organism.
previousIdentifications.comments: Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space (` | `).
previousIdentifications.examples: `Chalepidae`; `Pinus abies`; `Anthus sp., field ID by G. Iglesias | Anthus correndera, expert ID by C. Cicero 2009-02-12 based on morphology`

organismRemarks.name: organismRemarks
organismRemarks.label: Organism Remarks
organismRemarks.dc:description: Comments or notes about the dwc:Organism instance.

causeOfDeath.name: causeOfDeath
causeOfDeath.label: Cause Of Death
causeOfDeath.dc:description: An indication of the known or suspected cause of death of a dwc:Organism.
causeOfDeath.comments: The cause may be due to natural causes (e.g., disease, predation), human-related activities (e.g., roadkill, pollution), or other environmental factors (e.g., extreme weather events).

materialEntityID.name: materialEntityID
materialEntityID.label: Material Entity ID
materialEntityID.dc:description: An identifier for a particular instance of a dwc:MaterialEntity.
materialEntityID.comments: Values of dwc:materialEntityID are intended to uniquely and persistently identify a particular dwc:MaterialEntity within some context. Examples of context include a particular sample collection, an organization, or the worldwide scale. Recommended best practice is to use a persistent, globally unique identifier. The identifier is bound to a physical object (the dwc:MaterialEntity) as opposed to a particular digital record (representation) of that physical object.

digitalSpecimenID.name: digitalSpecimenID
digitalSpecimenID.label: Digital Specimen Identifier
digitalSpecimenID.dc:description: An identifier for a particular instance of a Digital Specimen.
digitalSpecimenID.comments: A Digital Specimen is defined in https://doi.org/10.3897/rio.7.e67379. A dwc:digitalSpecimenID is intended to uniquely and persistently identify a Digital Specimen. Recommended best practice is to use a DOI with machine readable metadata in the DOI record that uses a community agreed metadata profile (also known as FDO profile) for a Digital Specimen. For an example see: https://doi.org/10.3535/N75-CR4-0SM?noredirect. The identifier is for a digital information artifact (the Digital Specimen) as opposed to an identifier for a specific instance of a dwc:MaterialEntity.

materialEntityType.name: materialEntityType
materialEntityType.label: Material Entity Type
materialEntityType.dc:description: A category that best matches the nature of a dwc:MaterialEntity.
materialEntityType.comments: A more generic classification of a dwc:MaterialEntity than dwc:preparations. Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary. This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.
materialEntityType.examples: `Macro-object`; `Micro-object`; `Oversized object`; `Cut/polished gemstone`; `Compound Specimen`; `Core`; `Mixed Materials`; `Environmental sample`; `Microscope slide`; `Spore print`; `Macrofossil`; `Mesofossil`; `Microfossil`; `Pinned object/specimen`; `Taxidermy mount`; `Blood sampling cards`; `Oversized fossil`; `Anthropogenic Artifact`

discipline.name: discipline
discipline.label: Discipline
discipline.dc:description: The primary branch or branches of knowledge represented by the record.
discipline.comments: This term can be used to classify records according to branches of knowledge. Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary and to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space ( | ).This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.  It is also recommended to use this field to describe specimenType in MIDS.

preparations.name: preparations
preparations.label: Preparations
preparations.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of preparations and preservation methods for a dwc:MaterialEntity.
preparations.comments: Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space ( | ). This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

disposition.name: disposition
disposition.label: Disposition
disposition.dc:description: The current state of a dwc:MaterialEntity with respect to a collection.
disposition.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary. This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

verbatimLabel.name: verbatimLabel
verbatimLabel.label: Verbatim Label
verbatimLabel.dc:description: The content of this term should include no embellishments, prefixes, headers or other additions made to the text. Abbreviations must not be expanded and supposed misspellings must not be corrected. Lines or breakpoints between blocks of text that could be verified by seeing the original labels or images of them may be used. Examples of material entities include preserved specimens, fossil specimens, and material samples. Best practice is to use UTF-8 for all characters. Best practice is to add comment “verbatimLabel derived from human transcription” in dwc:occurrenceRemarks.

associatedSequences.name: associatedSequences
associatedSequences.label: Associated Sequences
associatedSequences.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of identifiers (publication, global unique identifier, URI) of genetic sequence information associated with the dwc:MaterialEntity.

materialEntityRemarks.name: materialEntityRemarks
materialEntityRemarks.label: Material Entity Remarks
materialEntityRemarks.dc:description: Comments or notes about the dwc:MaterialEntity instance.
materialEntityRemarks.examples: `found in association with charred remains`; `some original fragments missing`

materialSampleID.name: materialSampleID
materialSampleID.label: Material Sample ID
materialSampleID.dc:description: An identifier for the dwc:MaterialSample (as opposed to a particular digital record of the dwc:MaterialSample). In the absence of a persistent global unique identifier, construct one from a combination of identifiers in the record that will most closely make the dwc:materialSampleID globally unique.
materialSampleID.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a persistent, globally unique identifier.

eventID.name: eventID
eventID.label: Event ID
eventID.dc:description: An identifier for the set of information associated with a dwc:Event (something that occurs at a place and time). May be a global unique identifier or an identifier specific to the data set.

parentEventID.name: parentEventID
parentEventID.label: Parent Event ID
parentEventID.dc:description: An identifier for the broader dwc:Event that groups this and potentially other dwc:Events.
parentEventID.comments: Use a globally unique identifier for a dwc:Event or an identifier for a dwc:Event that is specific to the data set.
parentEventID.examples: `A1` (parentEventID to identify the main Whittaker Plot in nested samples, each with its own eventID - `A1:1`, `A1:2`).

eventType.name: eventType
eventType.label: Event Type
eventType.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary. Regardless of the dwc:eventType, the interval of the dwc:Event can be captured in dwc:eventDate. This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

projectTitle.name: projectTitle
projectTitle.label: Project Title
projectTitle.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of titles or names for projects that contributed to a dwc:Event.
projectTitle.comments: Use this term to provide the official name or title of a project as it is commonly known and cited. Avoid abbreviations unless they are widely understood. The recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space ( | ).

projectID.name: projectID
projectID.label: Project ID
projectID.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of identifiers for projects that contributed to a dwc:Event.
projectID.comments: A projectID may be shared in multiple distinct datasets. The nature of the association can be described in the metadata project description element. This term should be used to provide a globally unique identifier (GUID) for a project, if available. This could be a DOI, URI, or any other persistent identifier that ensures a project can be uniquely distinguished from others. The recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space ( | ).

fundingAttribution.name: fundingAttribution
fundingAttribution.label: Funding Attribution
fundingAttribution.dc:description: Text description of organizations or individuals who funded the creation of the resource.
fundingAttribution.comments: Specify the full official name of the funding body. This should include the complete name without abbreviations, unless the abbreviation is an official and commonly recognized form (e.g., NSF for the National Science Foundation). The recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space ( | ).

fundingAttributionID.name: fundingAttributionID
fundingAttributionID.label: Funding Attribution ID
fundingAttributionID.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of the globally unique identifiers for the funding organizations or agencies that supported the project.
fundingAttributionID.comments: Provide a unique identifier for the funding body, such as an identifier used in governmental or international databases. If no official identifier exists, use a persistent and unique identifier within your organization or dataset. The recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space ( | ).

fieldNumber.name: fieldNumber
fieldNumber.label: Field Number
fieldNumber.dc:description: An identifier given to the dwc:Event in the field. Often serves as a link between field notes and the dwc:Event.
fieldNumber.comments: This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

eventDate.name: eventDate
eventDate.label: Event Date
eventDate.dc:description: The date-time or interval during which a dwc:Event occurred. For occurrences, this is the date-time when the dwc:Event was recorded. Not suitable for a time in a geological context.
eventDate.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a date that conforms to ISO 8601-1:2019.
eventDate.examples: `1963-03-08T14:07-06:00` (8 Mar 1963 at or after 2:07pm and before 2:08pm in the time zone six hours earlier than UTC); `2009-02-20T08:40Z` (20 February 2009 at or after 8:40am and before 8:41 UTC); `2018-08-29T15:19` (29 August 2018 at or after 3:19pm and before 3:20pm local time); `1809-02-12` (within the day 12 February 1809); `1906-06` (in the month of June 1906); `1971` (in the year 1971); `2007-03-01T13:00:00Z/2008-05-11T15:30:00Z` (some time within the interval beginning 1 March 2007 at 1pm UTC and before 11 May 2008 at 3:30pm UTC); `1900/1909` (some time within the interval between the beginning of the year 1900 and before the year 1909); `2007-11-13/15` (some time in the interval between the beginning of 13 November 2007 and before 15 November 2007)

eventTime.name: eventTime
eventTime.label: Event Time
eventTime.dc:description: The time or interval during which a dwc:Event occurred.
eventTime.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a time of day that conforms to ISO 8601-1:2019.
eventTime.examples: `14:07-06:00` (at or after 2:07pm and before 2:08pm in the time zone six hours earlier than UTC); `08:40:21Z` (at or after 8:40:21am and before 8:41:22am UTC); `13:00:00Z/15:30:00Z` (at or after 1pm and before 3:30pm UTC)

startDayOfYear.name: startDayOfYear
startDayOfYear.label: Start Day Of Year
startDayOfYear.dc:description: The earliest integer day of the year on which the dwc:Event occurred (1 for January 1, 365 for December 31, except in a leap year, in which case it is 366).
startDayOfYear.examples: `1` (1 January); `366` (31 December); `365` (30 December in a leap year, 31 December in a non-leap year)

endDayOfYear.name: endDayOfYear
endDayOfYear.label: End Day Of Year
endDayOfYear.dc:description: The latest integer day of the year on which the dwc:Event occurred (1 for January 1, 365 for December 31, except in a leap year, in which case it is 366).
endDayOfYear.examples: `1` (1 January); `32` (1 February); `366` (31 December); `365` (30 December in a leap year, 31 December in a non-leap year)

year.name: year
year.label: Year
year.dc:description: The four-digit year in which the dwc:Event occurred, according to the Common Era Calendar.

validators.message: Year must be between 1600 and current year

month.name: month
month.label: Month
month.dc:description: The integer month in which the dwc:Event occurred.

day.name: day
day.label: Day
day.dc:description: The integer day of the month on which the dwc:Event occurred.

verbatimEventDate.name: verbatimEventDate
verbatimEventDate.label: Verbatim EventDate
verbatimEventDate.dc:description: The verbatim original representation of the date and time information for a dwc:Event.

habitat.name: habitat
habitat.label: Habitat
habitat.dc:description: A category or description of the habitat in which the dwc:Event occurred.
habitat.comments: This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

samplingProtocol.name: samplingProtocol
samplingProtocol.label: Sampling Protocol
samplingProtocol.dc:description: The names of, references to, or descriptions of the methods or protocols used during a dwc:Event.
samplingProtocol.comments: Recommended best practice is describe a dwc:Event with no more than one sampling protocol. In the case of a summary Event with multiple protocols, in which a specific protocol can not be attributed to specific dwc:Occurrences, the recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space (` | `). This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.
samplingProtocol.examples: `UV light trap`; `mist net`; `bottom trawl`; `ad hoc observation | point count`; `Penguins from space: faecal stains reveal the location of emperor penguin colonies, https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1466-8238.2009.00467.x`; `Takats et al. 2001. Guidelines for Nocturnal Owl Monitoring in North America. Beaverhill Bird Observatory and Bird Studies Canada, Edmonton, Alberta. 32 pp., http://www.bsc-eoc.org/download/Owl.pdf`

sampleSizeValue.name: sampleSizeValue
sampleSizeValue.label: Sample Size Value
sampleSizeValue.dc:description: A numeric value for a measurement of the size (time duration, length, area, or volume) of a sample in a sampling dwc:Event.
sampleSizeValue.comments: A dwc:sampleSizeValue must have a corresponding dwc:sampleSizeUnit.

sampleSizeUnit.name: sampleSizeUnit
sampleSizeUnit.label: Sample Size Unit
sampleSizeUnit.dc:description: The unit of measurement of the size (time duration, length, area, or volume) of a sample in a sampling dwc:Event.
sampleSizeUnit.comments: A dwc:sampleSizeUnit must have a corresponding dwc:sampleSizeValue, e.g., `5` for dwc:sampleSizeValue with `m` for dwc:sampleSizeUnit. This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

samplingEffort.name: samplingEffort
samplingEffort.label: Sampling Effort
samplingEffort.dc:description: The amount of effort expended during a dwc:Event.
samplingEffort.examples: `40 trap-nights`; `10 observer-hours`; `10 km by foot`; `30 km by car`

fieldNotes.name: fieldNotes
fieldNotes.label: Field Notes
fieldNotes.dc:description: One of a) an indicator of the existence of, b) a reference to (publication, URI), or c) the text of notes taken in the field about the dwc:Event.
fieldNotes.comments: This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

eventRemarks.name: eventRemarks
eventRemarks.label: Event Remarks
eventRemarks.dc:description: Comments or notes about the dwc:Event.
eventRemarks.examples: `After the recent rains the river is nearly at flood stage.`

locationID.name: locationID
locationID.label: Location ID
locationID.dc:description: An identifier for the set of dcterms:Location information. May be a global unique identifier or an identifier specific to the data set.

higherGeographyID.name: higherGeographyID
higherGeographyID.label: Higher Geography ID
higherGeographyID.dc:description: An identifier for the geographic region within which the dcterms:Location occurred.
higherGeographyID.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a persistent identifier from a controlled vocabulary such as the Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names.
higherGeographyID.examples: `http://vocab.getty.edu/tgn/1002002` (Antártida e Islas del Atlántico Sur, Territorio Nacional de la Tierra del Fuego, Argentina).

higherGeography.name: higherGeography
higherGeography.label: Higher Geography
higherGeography.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of geographic names less specific than the information captured in the dwc:locality term.
higherGeography.comments: Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space (` | `), with terms in order from least specific to most specific.
higherGeography.examples: `North Atlantic Ocean`; `South America | Argentina | Patagonia | Parque Nacional Nahuel Huapi | Neuquén | Los Lagos` with accompanying values `South America` (continent) `Argentina` (country), `Neuquén` (first order division), and `Los Lagos` (second order division)

continent.name: continent
continent.label: Continent
continent.dc:description: The name of the continent in which the dcterms:Location occurs.
continent.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as the Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names. Recommended best practice is to leave this field blank if the dcterms:Location spans multiple entities at this administrative level or if the dcterms:Location might be in one or another of multiple possible entities at this level. Multiplicity and uncertainty of the geographic entity can be captured either in the term dwc:higherGeography or in the term dwc:locality, or both.

waterBody.name: waterBody
waterBody.label: Water Body
waterBody.dc:description: The name of the water body in which the dcterms:Location occurs.
waterBody.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as the Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names.

islandGroup.name: islandGroup
islandGroup.label: Island Group
islandGroup.dc:description: The name of the island group in which the dcterms:Location occurs.
islandGroup.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as the Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names.

island.name: island
island.label: Island
island.dc:description: The name of the island on or near which the dcterms:Location occurs.
island.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as the Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names.

country.name: country
country.label: Country
country.dc:description: The name of the country or major administrative unit in which the dcterms:Location occurs.
country.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as the Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names. Recommended best practice is to leave this field blank if the dcterms:Location spans multiple entities at this administrative level or if the dcterms:Location might be in one or another of multiple possible entities at this level. Multiplicity and uncertainty of the geographic entity can be captured either in the term dwc:higherGeography or in the term dwc:locality, or both.

countryCode.name: countryCode
countryCode.label: Country Code
countryCode.dc:description: The standard code for the country in which the dcterms:Location occurs.
countryCode.comments: Recommended best practice is to use an ISO 3166-1-alpha-2 country code, or `ZZ` (for an unknown location or a location unassignable to a single country code), or `XZ` (for the high seas beyond national jurisdictions).

stateProvince.name: stateProvince
stateProvince.label: First Order Division
stateProvince.dc:description: The name of the next smaller administrative region than country (state, province, canton, department, region, etc.) in which the dcterms:Location occurs.
stateProvince.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as the Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names. Recommended best practice is to leave this field blank if the dcterms:Location spans multiple entities at this administrative level or if the dcterms:Location might be in one or another of multiple possible entities at this level. Multiplicity and uncertainty of the geographic entity can be captured either in the term dwc:higherGeography or in the term dwc:locality, or both.

county.name: county
county.label: Second Order Division
county.dc:description: The full, unabbreviated name of the next smaller administrative region than stateProvince (county, shire, department, etc.) in which the dcterms:Location occurs.
county.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as the Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names. Recommended best practice is to leave this field blank if the dcterms:Location spans multiple entities at this administrative level or if the dcterms:Location might be in one or another of multiple possible entities at this level. Multiplicity and uncertainty of the geographic entity can be captured either in the term dwc:higherGeography or in the term dwc:locality, or both.

municipality.name: municipality
municipality.label: Third Order Division
municipality.dc:description: The full, unabbreviated name of the next smaller administrative region than county (city, municipality, etc.) in which the dcterms:Location occurs. Do not use this term for a nearby named place that does not contain the actual dcterms:Location.
municipality.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as the Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names. Recommended best practice is to leave this field blank if the dcterms:Location spans multiple entities at this administrative level or if the dcterms:Location might be in one or another of multiple possible entities at this level. Multiplicity and uncertainty of the geographic entity can be captured either in the term dwc:higherGeography or in the term dwc:locality, or both.

locality.name: locality
locality.label: Locality
locality.dc:description: The specific description of the place.
locality.comments: Less specific geographic information can be provided in other geographic terms (dwc:higherGeography, dwc:continent, dwc:country, dwc:stateProvince, dwc:county, dwc:municipality, dwc:waterBody, dwc:island, dwc:islandGroup). This term may contain information modified from the original to correct perceived errors or standardize the description.
locality.examples: `Bariloche, 25 km NNE via Ruta Nacional 40 (=Ruta 237)`; `Queets Rainforest, Olympic National Park`

verbatimLocality.name: verbatimLocality
verbatimLocality.label: Verbatim Locality
verbatimLocality.dc:description: The original textual description of the place.
verbatimLocality.examples: `25 km NNE Bariloche por R. Nac. 237`

minimumElevationInMeters.name: minimumElevationInMeters
minimumElevationInMeters.label: Minimum Elevation In Meters
minimumElevationInMeters.dc:description: The lower limit of the range of elevation (altitude, usually above sea level), in meters.

maximumElevationInMeters.name: maximumElevationInMeters
maximumElevationInMeters.label: Maximum Elevation In Meters
maximumElevationInMeters.dc:description: The upper limit of the range of elevation (altitude, usually above sea level), in meters.

verbatimElevation.name: verbatimElevation
verbatimElevation.label: Verbatim Elevation
verbatimElevation.dc:description: The original description of the elevation (altitude, usually above sea level) of the Location.

verticalDatum.name: verticalDatum
verticalDatum.label: Vertical Datum
verticalDatum.dc:description: The vertical datum used as the reference upon which the values in the elevation terms are based.
verticalDatum.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary. This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

minimumDepthInMeters.name: minimumDepthInMeters
minimumDepthInMeters.label: Minimum Depth In Meters
minimumDepthInMeters.dc:description: The lesser depth of a range of depth below the local surface, in meters.

maximumDepthInMeters.name: maximumDepthInMeters
maximumDepthInMeters.label: Maximum Depth In Meters
maximumDepthInMeters.dc:description: The greater depth of a range of depth below the local surface, in meters.

verbatimDepth.name: verbatimDepth
verbatimDepth.label: Verbatim Depth
verbatimDepth.dc:description: The original description of the depth below the local surface.

minimumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters.name: minimumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters
minimumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters.label: Minimum Distance Above Surface In Meters
minimumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters.dc:description: The lesser distance in a range of distance from a reference surface in the vertical direction, in meters. Use positive values for locations above the surface, negative values for locations below. If depth measures are given, the reference surface is the location given by the depth, otherwise the reference surface is the location given by the elevation.
minimumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters.examples: `-1.5` (below the surface); `4.2` (above the surface); For a 1.5 meter sediment core from the bottom of a lake (at depth 20m) at 300m elevation: verbatimElevation: `300m` minimumElevationInMeters: `300`, maximumElevationInMeters: `300`, verbatimDepth: `20m`, minimumDepthInMeters: `20`, maximumDepthInMeters: `20`, minimumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters: `0`, maximumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters: `-1.5`.

maximumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters.name: maximumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters
maximumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters.label: Maximum Distance Above Surface In Meters
maximumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters.dc:description: The greater distance in a range of distance from a reference surface in the vertical direction, in meters. Use positive values for locations above the surface, negative values for locations below. If depth measures are given, the reference surface is the location given by the depth, otherwise the reference surface is the location given by the elevation.
maximumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters.examples: `-1.5` (below the surface); `4.2` (above the surface); For a 1.5 meter sediment core from the bottom of a lake (at depth 20m) at 300m elevation: verbatimElevation: `300m` minimumElevationInMeters: `300`, maximumElevationInMeters: `300`, verbatimDepth: `20m`, minimumDepthInMeters: `20`, maximumDepthInMeters: `20`, minimumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters: `0`, maximumDistanceAboveSurfaceInMeters: `-1.5`.

locationAccordingTo.name: locationAccordingTo
locationAccordingTo.label: Location According To
locationAccordingTo.dc:description: Information about the source of this dcterms:Location information. Could be a publication (gazetteer), institution, or team of individuals.
locationAccordingTo.comments: This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

locationRemarks.name: locationRemarks
locationRemarks.label: Location Remarks
locationRemarks.dc:description: Comments or notes about the dcterms:Location.

decimalLatitude.name: decimalLatitude
decimalLatitude.label: Decimal Latitude
decimalLatitude.dc:description: The geographic latitude (in decimal degrees, using the spatial reference system given in dwc:geodeticDatum) of the geographic center of a dcterms:Location. Positive values are north of the Equator, negative values are south of it. Legal values lie between -90 and 90, inclusive.

validators.message: Latitude must be between -90 and +90 degrees

decimalLongitude.name: decimalLongitude
decimalLongitude.label: Decimal Longitude
decimalLongitude.dc:description: The geographic longitude (in decimal degrees, using the spatial reference system given in dwc:geodeticDatum) of the geographic center of a dcterms:Location. Positive values are east of the Greenwich Meridian, negative values are west of it. Legal values lie between -180 and 180, inclusive.

validators.message: Longitude must be between -180 and +180 degrees

geodeticDatum.name: geodeticDatum
geodeticDatum.label: Geodetic Datum
geodeticDatum.dc:description: The ellipsoid, geodetic datum, or spatial reference system (SRS) upon which the geographic coordinates given in dwc:decimalLatitude and dwc:decimalLongitude are based.
geodeticDatum.comments: Recommended best practice is to use the EPSG code of the SRS, if known. Otherwise use a controlled vocabulary for the name or code of the geodetic datum, if known. Otherwise use a controlled vocabulary for the name or code of the ellipsoid, if known. If none of these is known, use the value `not recorded`. This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for a string literal value.

coordinateUncertaintyInMeters.name: coordinateUncertaintyInMeters
coordinateUncertaintyInMeters.label: Coordinate Uncertainty In Meters
coordinateUncertaintyInMeters.dc:description: The horizontal distance (in meters) from the given dwc:decimalLatitude and dwc:decimalLongitude describing the smallest circle containing the whole of the dcterms:Location. Leave the value empty if the uncertainty is unknown, cannot be estimated, or is not applicable (because there are no coordinates). Zero is not a valid value for this term.
coordinateUncertaintyInMeters.examples: `30` (reasonable lower limit on or after 2000-05-01 of a GPS reading under good conditions if the actual precision was not recorded at the time); `100` (reasonable lower limit before 2000-05-01 of a GPS reading under good conditions if the actual precision was not recorded at the time); `71` (uncertainty for a UTM coordinate having 100 meter precision and a known spatial reference system)

coordinatePrecision.name: coordinatePrecision
coordinatePrecision.label: Coordinate Precision
coordinatePrecision.dc:description: A decimal representation of the precision of the coordinates given in the dwc:decimalLatitude and dwc:decimalLongitude.
coordinatePrecision.examples: `0.00001` (normal GPS limit for decimal degrees); `0.000278` (nearest second); `0.01667` (nearest minute); `1.0` (nearest degree)

pointRadiusSpatialFit.name: pointRadiusSpatialFit
pointRadiusSpatialFit.label: Point Radius Spatial Fit
pointRadiusSpatialFit.dc:description: The ratio of the area of the point-radius (dwc:decimalLatitude, dwc:decimalLongitude, dwc:coordinateUncertaintyInMeters) to the area of the true (original, or most specific) spatial representation of the dcterms:Location. Legal values are 0, greater than or equal to 1, or undefined. A value of 1 is an exact match or 100% overlap. A value of 0 should be used if the given point-radius does not completely contain the original representation. The dwc:pointRadiusSpatialFit is undefined (and should be left empty) if the original representation is any geometry without area (e.g., a point or polyline) and without uncertainty and the given georeference is not that same geometry (without uncertainty). If both the original and the given georeference are the same point, the dwc:pointRadiusSpatialFit is 1.
pointRadiusSpatialFit.comments: Detailed explanations with graphical examples can be found in the Georeferencing Best Practices, Chapman and Wieczorek, 2020 (https://doi.org/10.15468/doc-gg7h-s853).

verbatimCoordinates.name: verbatimCoordinates
verbatimCoordinates.label: Verbatim Coordinates
verbatimCoordinates.dc:description: The verbatim original spatial coordinates of the dcterms:Location. The coordinate ellipsoid, geodeticDatum, or full Spatial Reference System (SRS) for these coordinates should be stored in dwc:verbatimSRS and the coordinate system should be stored in dwc:verbatimCoordinateSystem.

verbatimLatitude.name: verbatimLatitude
verbatimLatitude.label: Verbatim Latitude
verbatimLatitude.dc:description: The verbatim original latitude of the dcterms:Location. The coordinate ellipsoid, geodeticDatum, or full Spatial Reference System (SRS) for these coordinates should be stored in dwc:verbatimSRS and the coordinate system should be stored in dwc:verbatimCoordinateSystem.

validators.message: Latitude must be between -90 and +90 degrees

verbatimLongitude.name: verbatimLongitude
verbatimLongitude.label: Verbatim Longitude
verbatimLongitude.dc:description: The verbatim original longitude of the dcterms:Location. The coordinate ellipsoid, geodeticDatum, or full Spatial Reference System (SRS) for these coordinates should be stored in dwc:verbatimSRS and the coordinate system should be stored in dwc:verbatimCoordinateSystem.

validators.message: Longitude must be between -180 and +180 degrees

verbatimCoordinateSystem.name: verbatimCoordinateSystem
verbatimCoordinateSystem.label: Verbatim Coordinate System
verbatimCoordinateSystem.dc:description: The coordinate format for the dwc:verbatimLatitude and dwc:verbatimLongitude or the dwc:verbatimCoordinates of the dcterms:Location.
verbatimCoordinateSystem.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary. This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

verbatimSRS.name: verbatimSRS
verbatimSRS.label: Verbatim SRS
verbatimSRS.dc:description: The ellipsoid, geodetic datum, or spatial reference system (SRS) upon which coordinates given in dwc:verbatimLatitude and dwc:verbatimLongitude, or dwc:verbatimCoordinates are based.
verbatimSRS.comments: Recommended best practice is to use the EPSG code of the SRS, if known. Otherwise use a controlled vocabulary for the name or code of the geodetic datum, if known. Otherwise use a controlled vocabulary for the name or code of the ellipsoid, if known. If none of these is known, use the value `not recorded`. This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

footprintWKT.name: footprintWKT
footprintWKT.label: Footprint WKT
footprintWKT.dc:description: A Well-Known Text (WKT) representation of the shape (footprint, geometry) that defines the dcterms:Location. A dcterms:Location may have both a point-radius representation (see dwc:decimalLatitude) and a footprint representation, and they may differ from each other.
footprintWKT.comments: This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.
footprintWKT.examples: `POLYGON ((10 20, 11 20, 11 21, 10 21, 10 20))` (the one-degree bounding box with opposite corners at longitude=10, latitude=20 and longitude=11, latitude=21)

footprintSRS.name: footprintSRS
footprintSRS.label: Footprint SRS
footprintSRS.dc:description: The ellipsoid, geodetic datum, or spatial reference system (SRS) upon which the geometry given in dwc:footprintWKT is based.
footprintSRS.comments: Recommended best practice is to use the EPSG code of the SRS, if known. Otherwise use a controlled vocabulary for the name or code of the geodetic datum, if known. Otherwise use a controlled vocabulary for the name or code of the ellipsoid, if known. If none of these is known, use the value `not recorded`. It is also permitted to provide the SRS in Well-Known-Text, especially if no EPSG code provides the necessary values for the attributes of the SRS. Do not use this term to describe the SRS of the dwc:decimalLatitude and dwc:decimalLongitude, nor of any verbatim coordinates - use the dwc:geodeticDatum and dwc:verbatimSRS instead. This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.
footprintSRS.examples: `EPSG:4326`; `GEOGCS["GCS_WGS_1984", DATUM["D_WGS_1984", SPHEROID["WGS_1984",6378137,298.257223563]], PRIMEM["Greenwich",0], UNIT["Degree",0.0174532925199433]]` (WKT for the standard WGS84 Spatial Reference System EPSG:4326); `not recorded`

footprintSpatialFit.name: footprintSpatialFit
footprintSpatialFit.label: Footprint Spatial Fit
footprintSpatialFit.dc:description: The ratio of the area of the dwc:footprintWKT to the area of the true (original, or most specific) spatial representation of the dcterms:Location. Legal values are 0, greater than or equal to 1, or undefined. A value of 1 is an exact match or 100% overlap. A value of 0 should be used if the given dwc:footprintWKT does not completely contain the original representation. The dwc:footprintSpatialFit is undefined (and should be left empty) if the original representation is any geometry without area (e.g., a point or polyline) and without uncertainty and the given georeference is not that same geometry (without uncertainty). If both the original and the given georeference are the same point, the dwc:footprintSpatialFit is 1.
footprintSpatialFit.comments: Detailed explanations with graphical examples can be found in the Georeferencing Best Practices, Chapman and Wieczorek, 2020 (https://doi.org/10.15468/doc-gg7h-s853).

georeferencedBy.name: georeferencedBy
georeferencedBy.label: Georeferenced By
georeferencedBy.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of names of people, groups, or organizations who determined the georeference (spatial representation) for the dcterms:Location.
georeferencedBy.comments: Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space (` | `). This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

georeferencedDate.name: georeferencedDate
georeferencedDate.label: Georeferenced Date
georeferencedDate.dc:description: The date on which the dcterms:Location was georeferenced.
georeferencedDate.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a date that conforms to ISO 8601-1:2019.
georeferencedDate.examples: `1963-03-08T14:07-06:00` (8 Mar 1963 at or after 2:07pm and before 2:08pm in the time zone six hours earlier than UTC); `2009-02-20T08:40Z` (20 February 2009 at or after 8:40am and before 8:41 UTC); `2018-08-29T15:19` (29 August 2018 at or after 3:19pm and before 3:20pm local time); `1809-02-12` (within the day 12 February 1809); `1906-06` (in the month of June 1906); `1971` (in the year 1971); `2007-03-01T13:00:00Z/2008-05-11T15:30:00Z` (some time within the interval beginning 1 March 2007 at 1pm UTC and before 11 May 2008 at 3:30pm UTC); `1900/1909` (some time within the interval between the beginning of the year 1900 and before the year 1909); `2007-11-13/15` (some time in the interval between the beginning of 13 November 2007 and before 15 November 2007)

georeferenceProtocol.name: georeferenceProtocol
georeferenceProtocol.label: Georeference Protocol
georeferenceProtocol.dc:description: A description or reference to the methods used to determine the spatial footprint, coordinates, and uncertainties.
georeferenceProtocol.comments: This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.
georeferenceProtocol.examples: `Georeferencing Quick Reference Guide (Zermoglio et al. 2020, https://doi.org/10.35035/e09p-h128)`

georeferenceSources.name: georeferenceSources
georeferenceSources.label: Georeference Sources
georeferenceSources.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of maps, gazetteers, or other resources used to georeference the dcterms:Location, described specifically enough to allow anyone in the future to use the same resources.
georeferenceSources.comments: Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space (` | `). This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.
georeferenceSources.examples: `https://www.geonames.org/`; `USGS 1:24000 Florence Montana Quad 1967 | Terrametrics 2008 on Google Earth`; `GeoLocate`

georeferenceRemarks.name: georeferenceRemarks
georeferenceRemarks.label: Georeference Remarks
georeferenceRemarks.dc:description: Comments or notes about the spatial description determination, explaining assumptions made in addition or opposition to the those formalized in the method referred to in dwc:georeferenceProtocol.

geologicalContextID.name: geologicalContextID
geologicalContextID.label: Geological Context ID
geologicalContextID.dc:description: An identifier for the set of information associated with a dwc:GeologicalContext (the location within a geological context, such as stratigraphy). May be a global unique identifier or an identifier specific to the data set.

earliestEonOrLowestEonothem.name: earliestEonOrLowestEonothem
earliestEonOrLowestEonothem.label: Earliest Eon Or Lowest Eonothem
earliestEonOrLowestEonothem.dc:description: The full name of the earliest possible geochronologic eon or lowest chrono-stratigraphic eonothem or the informal name ("Precambrian") attributable to the stratigraphic horizon from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

latestEonOrHighestEonothem.name: latestEonOrHighestEonothem
latestEonOrHighestEonothem.label: Latest Eon Or Highest Eonothem
latestEonOrHighestEonothem.dc:description: The full name of the latest possible geochronologic eon or highest chrono-stratigraphic eonothem or the informal name ("Precambrian") attributable to the stratigraphic horizon from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

earliestEraOrLowestErathem.name: earliestEraOrLowestErathem
earliestEraOrLowestErathem.label: Earliest Era Or Lowest Erathem
earliestEraOrLowestErathem.dc:description: The full name of the earliest possible geochronologic era or lowest chronostratigraphic erathem attributable to the stratigraphic horizon from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

latestEraOrHighestErathem.name: latestEraOrHighestErathem
latestEraOrHighestErathem.label: Latest Era Or Highest Erathem
latestEraOrHighestErathem.dc:description: The full name of the latest possible geochronologic era or highest chronostratigraphic erathem attributable to the stratigraphic horizon from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

earliestPeriodOrLowestSystem.name: earliestPeriodOrLowestSystem
earliestPeriodOrLowestSystem.label: Earliest Period Or Lowest System
earliestPeriodOrLowestSystem.dc:description: The full name of the earliest possible geochronologic period or lowest chronostratigraphic system attributable to the stratigraphic horizon from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

latestPeriodOrHighestSystem.name: latestPeriodOrHighestSystem
latestPeriodOrHighestSystem.label: Latest Period Or Highest System
latestPeriodOrHighestSystem.dc:description: The full name of the latest possible geochronologic period or highest chronostratigraphic system attributable to the stratigraphic horizon from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

earliestEpochOrLowestSeries.name: earliestEpochOrLowestSeries
earliestEpochOrLowestSeries.label: Earliest Epoch Or Lowest Series
earliestEpochOrLowestSeries.dc:description: The full name of the earliest possible geochronologic epoch or lowest chronostratigraphic series attributable to the stratigraphic horizon from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

latestEpochOrHighestSeries.name: latestEpochOrHighestSeries
latestEpochOrHighestSeries.label: Latest Epoch Or Highest Series
latestEpochOrHighestSeries.dc:description: The full name of the latest possible geochronologic epoch or highest chronostratigraphic series attributable to the stratigraphic horizon from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

earliestAgeOrLowestStage.name: earliestAgeOrLowestStage
earliestAgeOrLowestStage.label: Earliest Age Or Lowest Stage
earliestAgeOrLowestStage.dc:description: The full name of the earliest possible geochronologic age or lowest chronostratigraphic stage attributable to the stratigraphic horizon from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

latestAgeOrHighestStage.name: latestAgeOrHighestStage
latestAgeOrHighestStage.label: Latest Age Or Highest Stage
latestAgeOrHighestStage.dc:description: The full name of the latest possible geochronologic age or highest chronostratigraphic stage attributable to the stratigraphic horizon from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

lowestBiostratigraphicZone.name: lowestBiostratigraphicZone
lowestBiostratigraphicZone.label: Lowest Biostratigraphic Zone
lowestBiostratigraphicZone.dc:description: The full name of the lowest possible geological biostratigraphic zone of the stratigraphic horizon from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

highestBiostratigraphicZone.name: highestBiostratigraphicZone
highestBiostratigraphicZone.label: Highest Biostratigraphic Zone
highestBiostratigraphicZone.dc:description: The full name of the highest possible geological biostratigraphic zone of the stratigraphic horizon from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

lithostratigraphicTerms.name: lithostratigraphicTerms
lithostratigraphicTerms.label: Lithostratigraphic Terms
lithostratigraphicTerms.dc:description: The combination of all lithostratigraphic names for the rock from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

group.name: group
group.label: Group
group.dc:description: The full name of the lithostratigraphic group from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

formation.name: formation
formation.label: Formation
formation.dc:description: The full name of the lithostratigraphic formation from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

member.name: member
member.label: Member
member.dc:description: The full name of the lithostratigraphic member from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

bed.name: bed
bed.label: Bed
bed.dc:description: The full name of the lithostratigraphic bed from which the dwc:MaterialEntity was collected.

identificationID.name: identificationID
identificationID.label: Identification ID
identificationID.dc:description: An identifier for the dwc:Identification (the body of information associated with the assignment of a scientific name). May be a global unique identifier or an identifier specific to the data set.

verbatimIdentification.name: verbatimIdentification
verbatimIdentification.label: Verbatim Identification
verbatimIdentification.dc:description: A string representing the taxonomic identification as it appeared in the original record.
verbatimIdentification.comments: This term is meant to allow the capture of an unaltered original identification/determination, including identification qualifiers, hybrid formulas, uncertainties, etc. This term is meant to be used in addition to dwc:scientificName (and dwc:identificationQualifier etc.), not instead of it.
verbatimIdentification.examples: `Peromyscus sp.`; `Ministrymon sp. nov. 1`; `Anser anser × Branta canadensis`; `Pachyporidae?`; `Potentilla × pantotricha Soják`; `Aconitum pilipes × A. variegatum`; `Lepomis auritus x cyanellus`

identificationQualifier.name: identificationQualifier
identificationQualifier.label: Identification Qualifier
identificationQualifier.dc:description: A brief phrase or a standard term ("cf.", "aff.") to express the determiner's doubts about the dwc:Identification.
identificationQualifier.comments: This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.
identificationQualifier.examples: `aff. agrifolia var. oxyadenia` (for `Quercus aff. agrifolia var. oxyadenia` with accompanying values `Quercus` in genus, `agrifolia`  in specificEpithet, `oxyadenia`  in infraspecificEpithet, and `var.` in taxonRank); `cf. var. oxyadenia` (for `Quercus agrifolia cf. var. oxyadenia` with accompanying values `Quercus` in genus, `agrifolia` in specificEpithet, `oxyadenia` in infraspecificEpithet, and `var.` in taxonRank)

typeStatus.name: typeStatus
typeStatus.label: Type Status
typeStatus.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of nomenclatural types (type status, typified scientific name, publication) applied to the subject.
typeStatus.comments: Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space (` | `). This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.
typeStatus.examples: `holotype of Ctenomys sociabilis. Pearson O. P., and M. I. Christie. 1985. Historia Natural, 5(37):388`; `holotype of Pinus abies | holotype of Picea abies`

typifiedName.name: typifiedName
typifiedName.label: Typified Name
typifiedName.dc:description: A scientific name that is based on a type specimen.
typifiedName.comments: Recommended best practice is also to indicate the dwc:typeStatus of the specimen.

identifiedBy.name: identifiedBy
identifiedBy.label: Identified By
identifiedBy.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of names of people, groups, or organizations who assigned the dwc:Taxon to the subject.
identifiedBy.comments: When used in the context of an Event (such as in the Humboldt Extension), the subject consists of all of the dwc:Organisms related to the Event. Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space ( | ). This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

identifiedByID.name: identifiedByID
identifiedByID.label: Identified By ID
identifiedByID.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of the globally unique identifier for the person, people, groups, or organizations responsible for assigning the dwc:Taxon to the subject.
identifiedByID.comments: Recommended best practice is to provide a single identifier that disambiguates the details of the identifying agent. If a list is used, the order of the identifiers on the list should not be assumed to convey any semantics. Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space (` | `).
identifiedByID.examples: `https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1825-0097` (for an individual); `https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1825-0097 | https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1825-0098` (for a list of people)

dateIdentified.name: dateIdentified
dateIdentified.label: Date Identified
dateIdentified.dc:description: The date on which the subject was determined as representing the dwc:Taxon.
dateIdentified.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a date that conforms to ISO 8601-1:2019.
dateIdentified.examples: `1963-03-08T14:07-06:00` (8 Mar 1963 at or after 2:07pm and before 2:08pm in the time zone six hours earlier than UTC); `2009-02-20T08:40Z` (20 February 2009 at or after 8:40am and before 8:41 UTC); `2018-08-29T15:19` (29 August 2018 at or after 3:19pm and before 3:20pm local time); `1809-02-12` (within the day 12 February 1809); `1906-06` (in the month of June 1906); `1971` (in the year 1971); `2007-03-01T13:00:00Z/2008-05-11T15:30:00Z` (some time within the interval beginning 1 March 2007 at 1pm UTC and before 11 May 2008 at 3:30pm UTC); `1900/1909` (some time within the interval between the beginning of the year 1900 and before the year 1909); `2007-11-13/15` (some time in the interval between the beginning of 13 November 2007 and before 15 November 2007)

identificationReferences.name: identificationReferences
identificationReferences.label: Identification References
identificationReferences.dc:description: A list (concatenated and separated) of references (publication, global unique identifier, URI) used in the dwc:Identification.
identificationReferences.comments: When used in the context of an Event (such as in the Humboldt Extension), the subject consists of all of the dwc:Organisms related to the Event. Recommended best practice is to separate the values in a list with space vertical bar space ( | ).
identificationReferences.examples: `Aves del Noroeste Patagonico. Christie et al. 2004.`; `Stebbins, R. Field Guide to Western Reptiles and Amphibians. 3rd Edition. 2003. | Irschick, D.J. and Shaffer, H.B. (1997). The polytypic species revisited: Morphological differentiation among tiger salamanders (Ambystoma tigrinum) (Amphibia: Caudata). Herpetologica, 53(1), 30-49.`

identificationVerificationStatus.name: identificationVerificationStatus
identificationVerificationStatus.label: Identification Verification Status
identificationVerificationStatus.dc:description: A categorical indicator of the extent to which the taxonomic identification has been verified to be correct.
identificationVerificationStatus.comments: Recommended best practice is to use a controlled vocabulary such as that used in HISPID and ABCD. This term has an equivalent in the dwciri: namespace that allows only an IRI as a value, whereas this term allows for any string literal value.

identificationRemarks.name: identificationRemarks
identificationRemarks.label: Identification Remarks
identificationRemarks.dc:description: Comments or notes about the dwc:Identification.
identificationRemarks.examples: `Distinguished between Anthus correndera and Anthus hellmayri based on the comparative lengths of the uñas.`

taxonID.name: taxonID
taxonID.label: Taxon ID
taxonID.dc:description: An identifier for the set of dwc:Taxon information. May be a global unique identifier or an identifier specific to the data set.

scientificNameID.name: scientificNameID
scientificNameID.label: Scientific Name ID
scientificNameID.dc:description: An identifier for the nomenclatural (not taxonomic) details of a scientific name.

acceptedNameUsageID.name: acceptedNameUsageID
acceptedNameUsageID.label: Accepted Name Usage ID
acceptedNameUsageID.dc:description: An identifier for the name usage (documented meaning of the name according to a source) of the currently valid (zoological) or accepted (botanical) taxon.
