assembly_qual.dc:description: The assembly quality category is based on sets of criteria outlined for each assembly quality category. For MISAG/MIMAG; Finished: Single, validated, contiguous sequence per replicon without gaps or ambiguities with a consensus error rate equivalent to Q50 or better. High Quality Draft:Multiple fragments where gaps span repetitive regions. Presence of the 23S, 16S and 5S rRNA genes and at least 18 tRNAs. Medium Quality Draft:Many fragments with little to no review of assembly other than reporting of standard assembly statistics. Low Quality Draft:Many fragments with little to no review of assembly other than reporting of standard assembly statistics. Assembly statistics include, but are not limited to total assembly size, number of contigs, contig N50/L50, and maximum contig length. For MIUVIG; Finished: Single, validated, contiguous sequence per replicon without gaps or ambiguities, with extensive manual review and editing to annotate putative gene functions and transcriptional units. High-quality draft genome: One or multiple fragments, totaling ≥ 90% of the expected genome or replicon sequence or predicted complete. Genome fragment(s): One or multiple fragments, totalling < 90% of the expected genome or replicon sequence, or for which no genome size could be estimated

assembly_name.name: assembly_name
assembly_name.label: Assembly_name
assembly_name.dc:description: Name/version of the assembly provided by the submitter that is used in the genome browsers and in the community

assembly_software.name: assembly_software
assembly_software.label: Assembly_software
assembly_software.dc:description: Tool(s) used for assembly, including version number and parameters

annot.name: annot
annot.label: Annot
annot.dc:description: Tool used for annotation, or for cases where annotation was provided by a community jamboree or model organism database rather than by a specific submitter

number_contig.name: number_contig
number_contig.label: Number_contig
number_contig.dc:description: Total number of contigs in the cleaned/submitted assembly that makes up a given genome, SAG, MAG, or UViG

feat_pred.name: feat_pred
feat_pred.label: Feat_pred
feat_pred.dc:description: Method used to predict UViGs features such as ORFs, integration site, etc.

ref_db.name: ref_db
ref_db.label: Ref_db
ref_db.dc:description: List of database(s) used for ORF annotation, along with version number and reference to website or publication

sim_search_meth.name: sim_search_meth
sim_search_meth.label: Sim_search_meth
sim_search_meth.dc:description: Tool used to compare ORFs with database, along with version and cutoffs used

tax_class.name: tax_class
tax_class.label: Tax_class
tax_class.dc:description: Method used for taxonomic classification, along with reference database used, classification rank, and thresholds used to classify new genomes
tax_class.examples: vConTACT vContact2 (references from NCBI RefSeq v83, genus rank classification, default parameters)

_16s_recover.name: _16s_recover
_16s_recover.label: _16s_recover
_16s_recover.dc:description: Can a 16S gene be recovered from the submitted SAG or MAG?

_16s_recover_software.name: _16s_recover_software
_16s_recover_software.label: _16s_recover_software
_16s_recover_software.dc:description: Tools used for 16S rRNA gene extraction

trnas.name: trnas
trnas.label: Trnas
trnas.dc:description: The total number of tRNAs identified from the SAG or MAG

compl_score.name: compl_score
compl_score.label: Compl_score
compl_score.dc:description: Completeness score is typically based on either the fraction of markers found as compared to a database or the percent of a genome found as compared to a closely related reference genome. High Quality Draft: >90%, Medium Quality Draft: >50%, and Low Quality Draft: < 50% should have the indicated completeness scores

compl_software.name: compl_software
compl_software.label: Compl_software
compl_software.dc:description: Tools used for completion estimate, i.e. checkm, anvi´o, busco

compl_appr.name: compl_appr
compl_appr.label: Compl_appr
compl_appr.dc:description: The approach used to determine the completeness of a given SAG or MAG, which would typically make use of a set of conserved marker genes or a closely related reference genome. For UViG completeness, include reference genome or group used, and contig feature suggesting a complete genome
compl_appr.examples: other: UViG length compared to the average length of reference genomes from the P22virus genus (NCBI RefSeq v83)

contam_score.name: contam_score
contam_score.label: Contam_score
contam_score.dc:description: The contamination score is based on the fraction of single-copy genes that are observed more than once in a query genome. The following scores are acceptable for; High Quality Draft: < 5%, Medium Quality Draft: < 10%, Low Quality Draft: < 10%. Contamination must be below 5% for a SAG or MAG to be deposited into any of the public databases

contam_screen_input.name: contam_screen_input
contam_screen_input.label: Contam_screen_input
contam_screen_input.dc:description: The type of sequence data used as input

contam_screen_param.name: contam_screen_param
contam_screen_param.label: Contam_screen_param
contam_screen_param.dc:description: Specific parameters used in the decontamination sofware, such as reference database, coverage, and kmers. Combinations of these parameters may also be used, i.e. kmer and coverage, or reference database and kmer

sort_tech.name: sort_tech
sort_tech.label: Sort_tech
sort_tech.dc:description: Method used to sort/isolate cells or particles of interest

single_cell_lysis_appr.name: single_cell_lysis_appr
single_cell_lysis_appr.label: Single_cell_lysis_appr
single_cell_lysis_appr.dc:description: Method used to free DNA from interior of the cell(s) or particle(s)

single_cell_lysis_prot.name: single_cell_lysis_prot
single_cell_lysis_prot.label: Single_cell_lysis_prot
single_cell_lysis_prot.dc:description: Name of the kit or standard protocol used for cell(s) or particle(s) lysis

wga_amp_appr.name: wga_amp_appr
wga_amp_appr.label: Wga_amp_appr
wga_amp_appr.dc:description: Method used to amplify genomic DNA in preparation for sequencing

wga_amp_kit.name: wga_amp_kit
wga_amp_kit.label: Wga_amp_kit
wga_amp_kit.dc:description: Kit used to amplify genomic DNA in preparation for sequencing

bin_param.name: bin_param
bin_param.label: Bin_param
bin_param.dc:description: The parameters that have been applied during the extraction of genomes from metagenomic datasets

bin_software.name: bin_software
bin_software.label: Bin_software
bin_software.dc:description: Tool(s) used for the extraction of genomes from metagenomic datasets

reassembly_bin.name: reassembly_bin
reassembly_bin.label: Reassembly_bin
reassembly_bin.dc:description: Has an assembly been performed on a genome bin extracted from a metagenomic assembly?

mag_cov_software.name: mag_cov_software
mag_cov_software.label: Mag_cov_software
mag_cov_software.dc:description: Tool(s) used to determine the genome coverage if coverage is used as a binning parameter in the extraction of genomes from metagenomic datasets

vir_ident_software.name: vir_ident_software
vir_ident_software.label: Vir_ident_software
vir_ident_software.dc:description: Tool(s) used for the identification of UViG as a viral genome, software or protocol name including version number, parameters, and cutoffs used

pred_genome_type.name: pred_genome_type
pred_genome_type.label: Pred_genome_type
pred_genome_type.dc:description: Type of genome predicted for the UViG

pred_genome_struc.name: pred_genome_struc
pred_genome_struc.label: Pred_genome_struc
pred_genome_struc.dc:description: Expected structure of the viral genome

otu_class_appr.name: otu_class_appr
otu_class_appr.label: Otu_class_appr
otu_class_appr.dc:description: Cutoffs and approach used when clustering new UViGs in "species-level" OTUs. Note that results from standard 95% ANI / 85% AF clustering should be provided alongside OTUS defined from another set of thresholds, even if the latter are the ones primarily used during the analysis

otu_seq_comp_appr.name: otu_seq_comp_appr
otu_seq_comp_appr.label: Otu_seq_comp_appr
otu_seq_comp_appr.dc:description: Tool and thresholds used to compare sequences when computing "species-level" OTUs

otu_db.name: otu_db
otu_db.label: Otu_db
otu_db.dc:description: Reference database (i.e. sequences not generated as part of the current study) used to cluster new genomes in "species-level" OTUs, if any

host_pred_appr.name: host_pred_appr
host_pred_appr.label: Host_pred_appr
host_pred_appr.dc:description: Tool or approach used for host prediction

host_pred_est_acc.name: host_pred_est_acc
host_pred_est_acc.label: Host_pred_est_acc
host_pred_est_acc.dc:description: For each tool or approach used for host prediction, estimated false discovery rates should be included, either computed de novo or from the literature
host_pred_est_acc.examples: CRISPR spacer match: 0 or 1 mismatches, estimated 8% FDR at the host genus rank (Edwards et al. 2016 doi:10.1093/femsre/fuv048)

sop.name: sop
sop.label: Sop
sop.dc:description: Standard operating procedures used in assembly and/or annotation of genomes, metagenomes or environmental sequences

pcr_primer_forward.name: pcr_primer_forward
pcr_primer_forward.label: Pcr_primer_forward
pcr_primer_forward.dc:description: Forward PCR primer that were used to amplify the sequence of the targeted gene, locus or subfragment. If multiple multiple forward or reverse primers are present in a single PCR reaction, there should be a full row for each of these linked to the same DWC Occurrence. The primer sequence should be reported in uppercase letters

pcr_primer_reverse.name: pcr_primer_reverse
pcr_primer_reverse.label: Pcr_primer_reverse
pcr_primer_reverse.dc:description: Reverse PCR primer that were used to amplify the sequence of the targeted gene, locus or subfragment. If multiple multiple forward or reverse primers are present in a single PCR reaction, there should be a full row for each of these linked to the same DWC Occurrence. The primer sequence should be reported in uppercase letters

pcr_primer_name_forward.name: pcr_primer_name_forward
pcr_primer_name_forward.label: Pcr_primer_name_forward
pcr_primer_name_forward.dc:description: Name of the forward PCR primer that were used to amplify the sequence of the targeted gene, locus or subfragment. If multiple multiple forward or reverse primers are present in a single PCR reaction, there should be a full row for each of these linked to the same DWC Occurrence.

pcr_primer_name_reverse.name: pcr_primer_name_reverse
pcr_primer_name_reverse.label: Pcr_primer_name_reverse
pcr_primer_name_reverse.dc:description: Name of the reverse PCR primer that were used to amplify the sequence of the targeted gene, locus or subfragment. If multiple multiple forward or reverse primers are present in a single PCR reaction, there should be a full row for each of these linked to the same DWC Occurrence.

pcr_primer_reference.name: pcr_primer_reference
pcr_primer_reference.label: Pcr_primer_reference
pcr_primer_reference.dc:description: Reference for the PCR primers that were used to amplify the sequence of the targeted gene, locus or subfragment.

methodDeterminationConcentrationAndRatio.name: methodDeterminationConcentrationAndRatios
methodDeterminationConcentrationAndRatio.label: Method Determination Concentration And Ratios
methodDeterminationConcentrationAndRatio.dc:description: Description of method used for concentration measurement

ratioOfAbsorbance260_230.name: ratioOfAbsorbance260_230
ratioOfAbsorbance260_230.label: Ratio Of Absorbance260_230
ratioOfAbsorbance260_230.dc:description: Ratio of absorbance at 260 nm and 230 nm assessing DNA purity (mostly secondary measure, indicates mainly EDTA, carbohydrates, phenol), (DNA samples only).

ratioOfAbsorbance260_280.name: ratioOfAbsorbance260_280
ratioOfAbsorbance260_280.label: Ratio Of Absorbance260_280
ratioOfAbsorbance260_280.dc:description: Ratio of absorbance at 280 nm and 230 nm assessing DNA purity (mostly secondary measure, indicates mainly EDTA, carbohydrates, phenol), (DNA samples only).

annealingTemp.name: annealingTemp
annealingTemp.label: Annealing Temp
annealingTemp.dc:description: The reaction temperature during the annealing phase of PCR.

annealingTempUnit.name: annealingTempUnit
annealingTempUnit.label: Annealing Temp Unit
annealingTempUnit.dc:description: Measurement unit of the reaction temperature during the annealing phase of PCR.

probeReporter.name: probeReporter
probeReporter.label: Probe Reporter
probeReporter.dc:description: Type of fluorophore (reporter) used. Probe anneals within amplified target DNA. Polymerase activity degrades the probe that has annealed to the template, and the probe releases the fluorophore from it and breaks the proximity to the quencher, thus allowing fluorescence of the fluorophore.

probeQuencher.name: probeQuencher
probeQuencher.label: Probe Quencher
probeQuencher.dc:description: Type of quencher used. The quencher molecule quenches the fluorescence emitted by the fluorophore when excited by the cycler’s light source As long as fluorophore and the quencher are in proximity, quenching inhibits any fluorescence signals.

ampliconSize.name: ampliconSize
ampliconSize.label: Amplicon Size
ampliconSize.dc:description: The length of the amplicon in basepairs.

thresholdQuantificationCycle.name: thresholdQuantificationCycle
thresholdQuantificationCycle.label: Threshold Quantification Cycle
thresholdQuantificationCycle.dc:description: Threshold for change in fluorescence signal between cycles

baselineValue.name: baselineValue
baselineValue.label: Baseline Value
baselineValue.dc:description: The number of cycles when fluorescence signal from the target amplification is below background fluorescence not originated from the real target amplification.

quantificationCycle.name: quantificationCycle
quantificationCycle.label: Quantification Cycle
quantificationCycle.dc:description: The number of cycles required for the fluorescent signal to cross a given value threshold above the baseline. Quantification cycle (Cq), threshold cycle (Ct), crossing point (Cp), and take-off point (TOP) refer to the same value from the real-time instrument. Use of quantification cycle (Cq), is preferable according to the RDML (Real-Time PCR Data Markup Language) data standard (http://www.rdml.org).

automaticThresholdQuantificationCycle.name: automaticThresholdQuantificationCycle
automaticThresholdQuantificationCycle.label: Automatic Threshold Quantification Cycle
automaticThresholdQuantificationCycle.dc:description: Whether the threshold was set by the instrument or manually.

automaticBaselineValue.name: automaticBaselineValue
automaticBaselineValue.label: Automatic Baseline Value
automaticBaselineValue.dc:description: Whether the baseline value was set by the instrument or manually.

contaminationAssessment.name: contaminationAssessment
contaminationAssessment.label: Contamination Assessment
contaminationAssessment.dc:description: Whether DNA or RNA contamination assessment was done or not.

partitionVolume.name: partitionVolume
partitionVolume.label: Partition Volume
partitionVolume.dc:description: An accurate estimation of partition volume. The sum of the partitions multiplied by the partition volume will enable the total volume of the reaction to be calculated.

estimatedNumberOfCopies.name: estimatedNumberOfCopies
estimatedNumberOfCopies.label: Estimated Number Of Copies
estimatedNumberOfCopies.dc:description: Number of target molecules per µl. Mean copies per partition (?) can be calculated using the number of partitions (n) and the estimated copy number in the total volume of all partitions (m) with a formula ?=m/n.

amplificationReactionVolumeUnit.name: amplificationReactionVolumeUnit
amplificationReactionVolumeUnit.label: Amplification Reaction Volume Unit
amplificationReactionVolumeUnit.dc:description: Unit used for PCR reaction volume. Many of the instruments require preparation of a much larger initial sample volume than is actually analyzed.

pcr_analysis_software.name: pcr_analysis_software
pcr_analysis_software.label: Pcr_analysis_software
pcr_analysis_software.dc:description: The program used to analyse the d(d)PCR runs.

experimentalVariance.name: experimentalVariance
experimentalVariance.label: Experimental Variance
experimentalVariance.dc:description: Multiple biological replicates are encouraged to assess total experimental variation. When single dPCR experiments are performed, a minimal estimate of variance due to counting error alone must be calculated from the binomial (or suitable equivalent) distribution.

pcr_primer_lod.name: pcr_primer_lod
pcr_primer_lod.label: Pcr_primer_lod
pcr_primer_lod.dc:description: The assay’s ability to detect the target at low levels.

pcr_primer_loq.name: pcr_primer_loq
pcr_primer_loq.label: Pcr_primer_loq
pcr_primer_loq.dc:description: The assay’s ability to quantify copy number at low levels.
